title: What “reliable” should mean for LLMs in life sciences
excerpt: Fluency is cheap and grounding is not. A short argument for treating evaluation as the product rather than an afterthought when language models touch regulated work.

pageSections.title: Reliable means grounded
pageSections.markdown:
When a language model writes a paragraph of a regulatory or scientific document, the failure that matters is rarely that the prose is awkward. The failure is that the prose can be confident and wrong.

That shifts evaluation from a clean-up step after the build into the thing being built. Reliability in these domains has to mean traceability, evidence, and constrained claims.

pageSections.quote: The model got less impressive and more trustworthy. That trade is the right one for this domain.
